question_title: Which of the following best describes the status of Djibouti’s April 10, 2026 presidential election before May 1, 2026?
summary: Djibouti's April 10, 2026 presidential election is 6 days away with status "Confirmed" by IFES. Incumbent Ismaïl Omar Guelleh has won every election since 1999 with overwhelming margins (97% in 2021). A constitutional amendment was passed in October 2025 specifically to remove the age limit so he could run for a 6th term. The electoral commission is dominated by loyalists, V-Dem classifies Djibouti as an "Electoral Autocracy," and the sole opposition candidate (Samatar/CDU) got 3% in 2021. No reports of postponement exist. Djibouti's small size means results are announced within days, and with 20 days between the election and the May 1 deadline, Wikipedia will almost certainly be updated in time. The only tail risks are a health emergency for the 78-year-old president or an unforeseen crisis.

factors.description: Guelleh has won every presidential election since 1999 with overwhelming margins (97% in 2021). Electoral autocracy with loyalist-controlled electoral commission.
factors.supports: Ismaïl Omar Guelleh officially declared winner

factors.description: Constitutional amendment passed Oct 2025 specifically to remove age limit for Guelleh to run. Entire state apparatus oriented toward his re-election.
factors.supports: Ismaïl Omar Guelleh officially declared winner

factors.description: Election confirmed by IFES, candidates filed, campaign underway, electoral commission installed. Only 6 days away with zero reports of postponement.
factors.supports: Ismaïl Omar Guelleh officially declared winner

factors.description: Small country with simple electoral system; results announced within days. 20 days between election and May 1 deadline gives ample time for Wikipedia update.
factors.supports: Ismaïl Omar Guelleh officially declared winner

factors.description: Only one nominal opposition candidate (Samatar/CDU) who got 3% in 2021. No realistic path to victory in authoritarian system.
factors.supports: Ismaïl Omar Guelleh officially declared winner

factors.description: Guelleh is 78 with recurring health concerns noted by Africa Center. A severe health crisis could theoretically force postponement.
Remote possibility Wikipedia does not update results in time, though 20 days and election notability make this very unlikely.
factors.supports: Vote held but no final official result before May 1

reasoning:
**Forecast: Ismaïl Omar Guelleh officially declared winner (93%)**

Key reasoning:
- **Election is 6 days away and confirmed** — IFES status is "Confirmed," candidates have filed, campaign is active, electoral commission installed
- **Guelleh dominates completely** — Won 97% in 2021, controls the electoral commission, and the constitutional amendment was engineered specifically for his candidacy
- **Only nominal opposition** — Samatar (CDU) got 3% last time; major opposition parties have boycotted since 2016
- **Ample time for resolution** — 20 days between the April 10 election and May 1 deadline; Djibouti's small size means results within days
- **Tail risks are small** — Health emergency (age 78) or unforeseen crisis could cause postponement (~4%), but no current indicators support this
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Which of the following best describes the status of Djibouti’s April 10, 2026 presidential election before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43053)
[Djibouti: An Uncontested Election Masks a Sea of Instability](https://africacenter.org/spotlight/en-elections-2026/djibouti/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option Ismaïl Omar Guelleh officially declared winner if the final result on the relevant election page shows him as the winner before May 1, 2026. This question resolves to the option Another candidate officially declared winner if a different candidate is shown as the winner before May 1, 2026. This question resolves to the option Vote held but no final official result before May 1 if voting occurs but no final winner is available by then. This question resolves to the option Election postponed or cancelled if the election does not take place as scheduled or is formally postponed, cancelled, or suspended before May 1, 2026. The primary source is Wikipedia’s 2026 Djiboutian presidential election page at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Djiboutian_presidential_election
fine_print: If the election goes to a second round that is not completed before May 1, 2026, resolve as Vote held but no final official result before May 1, 2026. If Wikipedia is unavailable or materially incomplete, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: IFES Election Guide Djibouti page https://www.electionguide.org/countries/id/60/ ; IFES Djibouti election page https://electionguide.org/elections/id/5061/
